cmd/balance-worker.purpose: Binary entrypoint for the balance-worker: subscribes to Kafka system and ingest topics via Watermill, recalculates metered entitlement grant burn-down using the credit engine and ClickHouse usage queries, and dispatches balance threshold notifications. All business logic lives in openmeter/entitlement/balanceworker; this folder only wires and starts it.

patterns.name: Wire-only entrypoint
patterns.description: wire.go declares the Application struct and the wire.Build call; wire_gen.go is fully generated. main.go only parses config and calls initializeApplication, app.SetGlobals(), app.Migrate(ctx), app.Run() — no business logic.
patterns.example: app, cleanup, err := initializeApplication(ctx, conf); defer cleanup(); app.SetGlobals(); app.Migrate(ctx); app.Run()

patterns.name: Shared lifecycle embedding
patterns.description: Application embeds common.GlobalInitializer, common.Migrator, and common.Runner — all lifecycle methods (SetGlobals, Migrate, Run) come from those shared structs, never defined locally.
patterns.example: type Application struct { common.GlobalInitializer; common.Migrator; common.Runner; Logger *slog.Logger; RuntimeMetricsCollector common.RuntimeMetricsCollector }

patterns.name: Config FieldsOf extraction
patterns.description: Worker-specific sub-config slices are extracted via wire.FieldsOf before being passed to provider sets, so the composite sets receive precisely the config struct they expect.

patterns.name: Binary-local metadata function
patterns.description: Each binary defines a local metadata() function passing version and the literal binary name to common.NewMetadata; the name string appears in telemetry labels.
patterns.example: func metadata(conf config.Configuration) common.Metadata { return common.NewMetadata(conf, version, "balance-worker") }

patterns.name: Version via ldflags + runtime/debug fallback
patterns.description: version/revision/revisionDate are provisioned by ldflags at build time; init() falls back to debug.ReadBuildInfo() vcs settings when ldflags are absent.
patterns.example: var version string; func init() { if version == "" { version = "unknown" } ; buildInfo, _ := debug.ReadBuildInfo() ... }

key_file_guides.role: Declares the Application struct fields and all common.* provider sets passed to wire.Build. Edit here when adding a new service dependency.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Has build tag //go:build wireinject — never remove it. Adding a domain service field requires also adding its provider set to wire.Build.

key_file_guides.role: Generated by `go generate` (Wire). Contains the full initializeApplication wiring sequence and cleanup chain.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Carries //go:build !wireinject and 'DO NOT EDIT' header — any manual edit is overwritten on the next `make generate`.

key_file_guides.role: Parses pflag/viper config and invokes lifecycle in order: parse flags -> unmarshal -> Validate -> initializeApplication -> SetGlobals -> Migrate -> Run.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add business logic here. app.Migrate(ctx) must precede app.Run(). ctx is context.Background() only because this is program root.

key_file_guides.role: Holds ldflags-provisioned version and reads vcs.revision / vcs.time from build info in init().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Defaults all three vars to 'unknown' when build info is missing; do not rely on them being populated in tests.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic to main.go instead of openmeter/entitlement/balanceworker
Removing the //go:build wireinject tag from wire.go
Adding a new domain service dependency without adding its provider set to wire.Build
Introducing context.Background() for anything other than the root ctx at program start

decisions.decision: common.Runner encapsulates the oklog/run group; main.go never builds run.Group directly.
decisions.rationale: Keeps shutdown ordering, signal handling, and goroutine lifecycle consistent across all workers without duplicating boilerplate.

decisions.decision: BalanceWorker wires Notification, Svix, and Subject services even though it serves no HTTP.
decisions.rationale: Balance threshold events are dispatched to notification.Service after burn-down recalculation, requiring the full Svix/notification plumbing in this binary.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new provider set to the balance-worker DI graph

cmd/billing-worker.purpose: Binary entrypoint for the billing-worker: subscribes to Kafka system events via Watermill and processes subscription sync, invoice creation, and charge advancement events. Performs post-migration provisioning (ledger business accounts, sandbox app) before starting the run loop.

patterns.name: Post-migration provisioning before Run
patterns.description: main.go calls app.LedgerAccountResolver.EnsureBusinessAccounts and app.AppRegistry.SandboxProvisioner after Migrate and strictly before Run — ordering is critical for correct startup state.

patterns.name: Extended Application struct for post-Migrate provisioning
patterns.description: Beyond the shared GlobalInitializer/Migrator/Runner embeds, Application exposes AppRegistry, LedgerAccountResolver, NamespaceManager, Streaming, and Meter as fields so main.go can drive provisioning.
patterns.example: type Application struct { common.GlobalInitializer; common.Migrator; common.Runner; AppRegistry common.AppRegistry; LedgerAccountResolver ledger.AccountResolver; NamespaceManager *namespace.Manager; Streaming streaming.Connector; ... }

patterns.name: BillingWorker composite provider set
patterns.description: wire.Build uses common.BillingWorker which internally wires billing adapter, rating service, subscription sync, charges, and the ledger stack. Individual billing sub-services are not listed separately.
patterns.example: common.BillingWorker // wires billingRegistry, subscriptionsyncService, workerOptions, worker in sequence

patterns.name: Config FieldsOf extraction for worker sub-config
patterns.description: wire.FieldsOf extracts the consumer/worker config slices for Wire from BillingWorkerConfiguration and BillingConfiguration.

patterns.name: FeatureGateNoopSet wiring
patterns.description: wire.Build includes common.FeatureGateNoopSet so featuregate.NewNoop() is injected; the billing-worker runs with a noop feature gate rather than a live gate provider.

key_file_guides.role: Startup sequence: parse config -> initializeApplication -> SetGlobals -> Migrate -> EnsureBusinessAccounts -> SandboxProvisioner -> Run.
key_file_guides.watch_for: EnsureBusinessAccounts and SandboxProvisioner must run after Migrate but before Run. Any new post-migration provisioning must respect this ordering and use GetDefaultNamespace().

key_file_guides.role: Declares the extended Application struct and all provider sets including common.BillingWorker, common.TaxCode, and common.FeatureGateNoopSet.
key_file_guides.watch_for: common.BillingWorker is a composite set — don't duplicate the billing sub-services it already includes. Has //go:build wireinject; never remove.

key_file_guides.role: Generated — shows the full wiring sequence including ledger stack (NewLedgerHistoricalRepo, NewLedgerAccountService, ...), AppRegistry, and Stripe/CustomInvoicing/Sandbox apps.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DO NOT EDIT. The ledger stack wiring is gated by creditsConfiguration — verify the credits path when debugging ledger writes.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic to main.go — it belongs in openmeter/billing/worker or openmeter/billing/worker/subscriptionsync
Calling EnsureBusinessAccounts or SandboxProvisioner after Run() — they must execute before the worker starts consuming events
Duplicating billing sub-service provider sets already included by common.BillingWorker
Bypassing the credits.enabled guard by directly constructing ledger services outside common.LedgerStack

decisions.decision: Ledger business accounts and the sandbox app are provisioned in billing-worker.
decisions.rationale: When billing-worker runs independently of cmd/server it must self-provision its required DB state before consuming Kafka events.

decisions.decision: AppRegistry (Sandbox, Stripe, CustomInvoicing) is wired in billing-worker.
decisions.rationale: Invoice state-machine events dispatch to the registered InvoicingApp, so app implementations must be registered before the Watermill router starts consuming.

code_examples.scenario: Correct post-Migrate startup sequence in billing-worker main.go

cmd/jobs/billing/advance.purpose: Cobra sub-command package for invoice advance operations (list, advance single invoice, advance all). All service access flows exclusively through the internal.App.BillingAutoAdvancer singleton wired by app/common; no local service construction is permitted.

patterns.name: internal.App singleton access
patterns.description: All service calls go through internal.App.<Field>; never construct services locally. The singleton is populated by Wire before Cobra executes.

patterns.name: cmd.Context() for context propagation
patterns.description: Always pass cmd.Context() to service calls — never context.Background() — so cancellation and deadlines from Cobra's context chain are respected.

patterns.name: Sub-command factory functions
patterns.description: Each sub-command is a `var <Name>Cmd = func() *cobra.Command { ... }` factory rather than a top-level var, allowing deferred flag registration and avoiding init-order issues.
patterns.example: var ListCmd = func() *cobra.Command { cmd := &cobra.Command{Use: "list", ...}; return cmd }

patterns.name: PersistentFlags on parent Cmd
patterns.description: Flags shared across sub-commands (e.g. --namespace) are registered on the parent Cmd via Cmd.PersistentFlags() in init(), not on individual sub-commands.

patterns.name: Nil slice for optional namespace filter
patterns.description: The namespace filter is a []string built conditionally so the service receives nil (unfiltered) when no namespace is specified, not an empty slice.
patterns.example: var ns []string; if namespace != "" { ns = append(ns, namespace) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines the parent Cmd plus ListCmd, InvoiceCmd, and AllCmd factory functions for invoice advance operations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: InvoiceCmd validates namespace is non-empty before calling AdvanceInvoice; replicate this guard on any new namespace-scoped sub-command. AllCmd registers its own --batch flag on cmd.PersistentFlags(), not the parent.

anti_patterns: Constructing billing services locally instead of using internal.App
Registering sub-command-specific flags on the parent Cmd (pollutes sibling commands)
Hardcoding namespace strings instead of accepting them via the --namespace flag
Calling AdvanceInvoice without first validating that namespace is non-empty

decisions.decision: Thin Cobra wrappers delegate to internal.App service fields.
decisions.rationale: Keeps cmd/* free of business logic; all wiring happens in app/common via Wire so each sub-command is independently testable and the DI graph is compile-time verified.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new advance sub-command scoped to a customer
